name: Add TypeScript Type Checking to CI/CD
description: Configure continuous integration to run TypeScript type checking on every pull request, catching type errors before they reach production.

steps.title: Create a GitHub Actions workflow
steps.description: Add a workflow that runs type checking on every push and pull request.
steps.command:
mkdir -p .github/workflows && cat > .github/workflows/typecheck.yml << 'EOF'
name: Type Check
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  typecheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm tsc --noEmit
EOF

steps.title: Add a type-check npm script
steps.description: Add a dedicated script that CI and developers can use consistently.

steps.title: Add a pre-commit hook with Husky
steps.description: Run type checking locally before commits reach CI.
steps.command: pnpm add -D husky && npx husky init && echo 'pnpm typecheck' > .husky/pre-commit
steps.tip: For faster local feedback, use lint-staged to only type-check changed files, though tsc --noEmit checks the whole project regardless.

steps.title: Verify the CI workflow runs correctly
steps.description: Push the workflow file and check that it triggers on the next PR.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# TypeScript CI Type Checking Setup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Creating GitHub Actions workflow ==="
mkdir -p .github/workflows

cat > .github/workflows/typecheck.yml << 'EOF'
name: Type Check & Lint
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  quality:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Type Check
        run: pnpm tsc --noEmit
      - name: Lint
        run: pnpm eslint .
EOF

echo "=== Adding scripts ==="
npm pkg set scripts.typecheck="tsc --noEmit"
npm pkg set scripts.ci="pnpm typecheck && pnpm eslint ."

echo "=== Workflow created at .github/workflows/typecheck.yml ==="

faq.question: Should I run tsc --noEmit or tsc --build in CI?
faq.answer: Use tsc --noEmit for single-package projects (fastest). Use tsc --build for monorepos with project references, as it type-checks packages in dependency order and supports incremental compilation.

faq.question: How can I speed up type checking in CI?
faq.answer: Cache node_modules and .tsbuildinfo files between runs. Use incremental: true in tsconfig.json. For monorepos, use tsc --build which only rechecks changed packages.
